title: Axion electron-electron interaction in the RaOH molecule to search for Dark matter
abstract: Axions are promising candidates for the role of Dark matter particles. In this paper, the question of the suitability of the RaOH molecule for the experimental detection of electron-electron interactions through the exchange of axions is studied. To take into account an impact of the rotations and vibrations a computation must be performed for a large number of molecular configurations. In this work we study this problem using a combination of the Generalized relativistic effective core potential and One-center restoration technique of the correct four-component spinors.
summary: This paper calculates the sensitivity of the RaOH molecule to axion-mediated electron-electron interactions. By using advanced relativistic electronic structure methods, the authors provide a theoretical basis for using heavy-atom molecules to search for dark matter signatures through parity and time-reversal symmetry violation.
main_problem: Investigating the suitability of the RaOH molecule for detecting axion-mediated electron-electron interactions as a signature of dark matter.
main_result: The study establishes a theoretical framework to calculate the sensitivity parameter (W_ee) for axion-mediated interactions in RaOH, providing comparative values against other molecules like YbOH and BaF.
method: Relativistic ab initio electronic structure calculations using the DIRAC19 package, combining Generalized Relativistic Effective Core Potentials (GRECP) with a one-center restoration technique.
model_or_system: The system is the triatomic RaOH molecule, modeled as a linear rotator with a heavy radium atom and a rigid OH ligand, subject to P and T-violating axion-mediated potentials.
key_observables: The sensitivity parameter (E_ax or W_ee) representing the expectation value of the P, T-odd Hamiltonian, and the energy shift in an external electric field.
important_parameters: Axion mass range of 10^-12 to 10^3 eV; coupling constants g_e,S and g_e,P; molecular angular momentum quantum numbers J, l, and s.
main_assumptions: The OH ligand is treated as a rigid rotor; the Yukawa potential is approximated by a 1/r potential; the Born-Oppenheimer and tree approximations are used.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows a Feynman diagram of the axion-mediated electron interaction; Figure 2 provides a structural representation of the RaOH molecule.
paper_structure: The paper progresses from the astrophysical context of dark matter to the specific quantum mechanical interaction model, followed by the description of relativistic computational methods and concluding with numerical results and comparisons.
why_interesting: This work is relevant to AMO physics as it utilizes precision molecular spectroscopy and relativistic molecular modeling to probe fundamental physics and search for new particles.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: Figure 1: Feynman diagram for the axion electron-electron interaction, the dotted line corresponds to the axion
